name: Viking 1 lander
fact: First fully successful Mars surface mission. Viking 1 operated for over six years on the surface — the longest-duration Mars lander mission until Opportunity broke the record in 2010. It returned the first colour images from the Martian surface, conducted the first life-detection experiments (results famously inconclusive), and characterised the regolith and weather.
capability: First long-duration in-situ Martian science — every modern lander runs on Viking's playbook.
